sourceMovie.title: Eraser
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Eraser works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Commuter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eraser clicked for you, The Commuter (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Legacy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eraser clicked for you, The Bourne Legacy (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eraser?
faq.answer: Try The Commuter, The Bourne Legacy, The Fugitive—each shares DNA with Eraser while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eraser?
faq.answer: Eraser (1996) is best described as Action, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eraser part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Eraser Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
